# Abstract
We fabricate carbon fiber composites with improved tensile strength. The process optimizes resin curing and fiber alignment.

# Introduction
Composite performance depends on microstructure and curing conditions. Small variations can lead to failure.

# Methods
Samples were produced with controlled temperature ramps and vacuum pressure. Tensile testing followed ASTM standards.

# Results
Strength increased by 12 percent and variability decreased by 30 percent. Fracture surfaces showed fewer voids.

Figure 1. Microstructure images of optimized composites.

# Discussion
Improved alignment reduces stress concentrations and crack initiation. Manufacturing scale up remains challenging.

Table 1. Mechanical properties across curing profiles.

# Conclusion
Process control enhances composite strength and reliability. Future work will integrate real time sensing.

# References
[1] Q. Materials. Carbon fiber curing techniques. 2012.
[2] R. Materials. Composite failure analysis. 2018.
